summary.intent: Build a browser-in-browser feature for moltis: live CDP screencasting with interactive mouse/keyboard/scroll control, session management UI, URL autocomplete, session history/action logging, and cookie persistence across restarts.
summary.outcome: Implemented the full feature end-to-end: live screencasting works, interactions (click/scroll/keyboard) relay to CDP, sessions show in UI with live/paused/creating states, action logging to SQLite, history panel for closed sessions, and shared cookie profile across sessions. PR #531 opened. Several polish bugs remain open.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService each create independent BrowserManager instances by default — sessions created by the tool are invisible to the gateway API unless they share an Arc<OnceCell<BrowserManager>>.
The v4 WebSocket protocol requires clients to explicitly subscribe to event types in a list built from Object.keys(eventHandlers) — browser.screencast.frame was missing, silently dropping all frames.
CDP screencast relay task exits immediately when tx.send() finds 0 receivers (lazybroadcast::error::SendError) — should drop frames instead of exiting so late subscribers still receive frames.
Browserless/Chrome sandbox containers use a session-scoped profile dir by default, so cookies never persist across restarts. Shared profile dir with SingletonLock cleanup enables cookie persistence.
The workspace enforces -D clippy::expect_used even in test code — must use unwrap_or_else or assert! patterns instead of .expect().
axum 0.8 changed route param syntax from :param to {param} — old syntax panics at startup.
moltis.toml config file values override code-level defaults — viewport 2560x1440@2x in the config negated the code default change to 1440x900.
Rapid CDP start_screencast/stop_screencast cycles kill the Chrome WebSocket connection — screencasts should persist across session switches and only stop on explicit close.

code.finding: LLMs (MiniMax in particular) send explicit null for every optional field. serde #[serde(default)] handles missing keys but not explicit null on u64 — obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) must be called before deserialization.
The relay task called tx.send() before any subscribers existed, got SendError (0 receivers), and exited. Fix: match on SendError, continue (drop frame) instead of break (exit task).
CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent with type=mouseWheel requires BOTH deltaX and deltaY to be present — conditional inclusion of zero values causes CDP to reject the event with a protocol error.

Canvas event listeners attached in useEffect([]) never fire when canvas is conditionally rendered — the effect runs once before the canvas exists. Must use a ref callback that fires whenever the DOM element appears.

Screencast metadata includes offset_top (height of browser chrome above the page). This must be added to y-coordinates when mapping canvas clicks to CDP coordinates, otherwise clicks appear higher than intended.

Per-session sandbox profile dirs (profile/sandbox/{session_id}/) give isolation but lose cookies on every new session. Shared profile dir with fs::remove_file on SingletonLock before container start enables cookie reuse.

subscribeEvents list is built from Object.keys(eventHandlers). browser.screencast.frame was not in this map, so the broadcast filter in broadcast.rs silently dropped every screencast frame before it reached the WebSocket client.
axum 0.8 requires {session_id} syntax for path params, not :session_id. Old syntax panics at server startup with a message about colon-prefixed segments.

workflow: When a broadcast pipeline works in unit tests but fails end-to-end, the subscription/filter layer is the prime suspect — add trace logging at each hop (CDP → relay → broadcast → WS client) to find where frames disappear.
Integration tests against a real CDP/browser (marked #[ignore]) are the most reliable way to catch CDP protocol requirements like the mouseWheel deltaX/deltaY constraint that unit tests would miss.
YubiKey-signed git pushes require interactive tap — background push tasks will fail with exit code 128; retry loop needed.

friction: YubiKey tap required for every push — multiple push failures in background tasks, requiring manual retry.
Pre-existing CUDA/voice clippy errors in the workspace made it hard to distinguish new errors from noise.
serde default behavior for explicit null vs missing keys is a non-obvious footgun that caused the initial crash — the fix is one line but required understanding the LLM behavior.
The screencast frame delivery bug required 4 separate debugging rounds across the CDP → relay → broadcast → WS subscription chain before the actual root cause (missing WS subscription) was found.
moltis.toml config file silently overriding code defaults meant the viewport fix appeared to work but didn't — required inspecting the user's config file directly.
axum 0.8 breaking change in route syntax caused a runtime panic (not compile error) that only appeared when starting the server.
open_items: Scrollbar overlay positioning is still broken — shows below the browser canvas instead of overlaid on it. Needs a different CSS layout approach (absolute positioning inside a relative wrapper that matches exact canvas dimensions).
Cookie persistence fix (shared sandbox profile dir) was pushed but not tested across a restart cycle.
WebRTC upgrade path identified (neko architecture, webrtc-rs) as the right long-term direction for sub-100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth efficiency — not implemented.
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast (eliminate base64 overhead, ~33% bandwidth reduction) identified but not implemented.
Session action log currently records navigate/close actions but does not capture screenshots at each step for visual replay.
PR #531 is open but not merged — includes ~30 commits across the full browser viewing feature.
The scrollbar click-to-scroll uses CDP executeScript to get scrollHeight/scrollTop — this polling approach is imprecise; a CDP Runtime event or Page lifecycle event would be more accurate.
Rapid session creation may still produce a race between placeholder removal and activeSession update causing the new session to appear unselected.
